headline: Grant and West Dive into Dark Financial Comedy 'The Man Who Stole Portugal'
lede: Richard E. Grant and Dominic West are set to headline a provocative new period heist drama that unravels one of the 20th century's most brazen financial cons.
body:
British acting heavyweights Richard E. Grant and Dominic West are diving deep into the murky waters of financial skullduggery with their upcoming dark comedy *The Man Who Stole Portugal*.

Director Thomas Napper, known for his gritty boxing drama *Jawbone*, will helm the project that explores a shocking real-life financial fraud perpetrated by Alves Reis in the early 20th century. The film promises a sardonic exploration of ambition, deception, and the audacious lengths one man will go to rewrite his financial destiny.

James Nelson Joyce leads the cast as Alves Reis, the central figure whose elaborate scheme attempted to essentially print his own national currency. Grant and West will provide critical supporting performances that are expected to inject dark humor into the narrative's complex moral landscape.

Principal photography has commenced, signaling the production's commitment to bringing this extraordinary true story to the screen. The project represents another fascinating entry in the growing genre of financial crime narratives that blend historical accuracy with compelling dramatic storytelling.
key_facts: Stars Richard E. Grant and Dominic West
